content:
You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the mbed-os codebase.
ARM Mbed OS is an embedded operating system for ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers. It includes a hardware abstraction layer (HAL), RTOS primitives, peripheral drivers (SPI, I2C, UART, CAN, USB), connectivity (BLE, Wi-Fi, cellular), storage, and security. C/C++ codebase.

TOOL USAGE — you MUST use these exact formats:

To run a shell command:
<bash>your_command_here</bash>

To view a file:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>

To view specific lines:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor> [start_line, end_line]

To edit a file (replace text):
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>exact text to replace</old_str>
<new_str>replacement text</new_str>

When finished, say: SUBMIT

IMPORTANT: Each response must contain EXACTLY ONE tool call. Do NOT put multiple tool calls in one response.

Working directory is /repo.
Think step by step. First understand the code, then investigate, then fix.

There is a peripheral initialization in wrong order causing dependent hardware to malfunction downstream of function ENET_Deinit in the targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS subsystem.
The function is located at targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c:344.
Please investigate and fix this issue.

I'll investigate the ENET_Deinit function in the fsl_enet.c file to identify the peripheral initialization order issue. Let me first look at the function to understand its current implementation.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c</str_replace_editor> [340, 360]

Let me examine more of the ENET_Deinit function and its surrounding context to better understand the initialization order issue. I'll look at a broader section of the file to see what other operations might be related.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c</str_replace_editor> [340, 380]

Let me check the ENET_Init function to understand the proper initialization sequence and compare it with the deinitialization sequence in ENET_Deinit.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c</str_replace_editor> [200, 300]

Let me find and examine the ENET_Init function to understand the proper initialization sequence.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c</str_replace_editor> [400, 500]

Let me continue looking at the ENET_Init function to see the complete initialization sequence.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c</str_replace_editor> [300, 400]

Now let me look at the ENET_Reset function to understand what it does during initialization.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c</str_replace_editor> [1, 100]

Let me search for the ENET_Reset function implementation:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c</str_replace_editor> [500, 600]

Let me search for the ENET_Reset function:

<bash>grep -n "ENET_Reset" /repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c</bash>

Exit code: 0
326:    ENET_Reset(base);

Let me search for the actual implementation of ENET_Reset:

<bash>grep -n "static.*ENET_Reset\|void.*ENET_Reset" /repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c</bash>
Exit code: 1

Let me search more broadly for reset functionality:

<bash>grep -n -i "reset" /repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c</bash>

Exit code: 0
325:    /* Reset ENET module. */
326:    ENET_Reset(base);
777:    /* Reset the length to zero. */

Let me check the header file to see the ENET_Reset declaration:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.h</str_replace_editor> [1, 100]

/*

#ifndef _FSL_ENET_H_
#define _FSL_ENET_H_

#include "fsl_common.h"

/*!
 * @addtogroup enet
 * @{
 */


/*******************************************************************************
 * Definitions
 ******************************************************************************/

/*! @name Driver version */
/*@{*/
/*! @brief Defines the driver version. */
#define FSL_ENET_DRIVER_VERSION (MAKE_VERSION(2, 0, 1)) /*!< Version 2.0.1. */
/*@}*/

/*! @name Control and status region bit masks of the receive buffer descriptor. */
/*@{*/
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_EMPTY_MASK 0x8000U       /*!< Empty bit mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_SOFTOWNER1_MASK 0x4000U  /*!< Software owner one mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_WRAP_MASK 0x2000U        /*!< Next buffer descriptor is the start address. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_SOFTOWNER2_Mask 0x1000U  /*!< Software owner two mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_LAST_MASK 0x0800U        /*!< Last BD of the frame mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_MISS_MASK 0x0100U        /*!< Received because of the promiscuous mode. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_BROADCAST_MASK 0x0080U   /*!< Broadcast packet mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_MULTICAST_MASK 0x0040U   /*!< Multicast packet mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_LENVLIOLATE_MASK 0x0020U /*!< Length violation mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_NOOCTET_MASK 0x0010U     /*!< Non-octet aligned frame mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_CRC_MASK 0x0004U         /*!< CRC error mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_OVERRUN_MASK 0x0002U     /*!< FIFO overrun mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_TRUNC_MASK 0x0001U       /*!< Frame is truncated mask. */
/*@}*/

/*! @name Control and status bit masks of the transmit buffer descriptor. */
/*@{*/
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_TX_READY_MASK 0x8000U       /*!< Ready bit mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_TX_SOFTOWENER1_MASK 0x4000U /*!< Software owner one mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_TX_WRAP_MASK 0x2000U        /*!< Wrap buffer descriptor mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_TX_SOFTOWENER2_MASK 0x1000U /*!< Software owner two mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_TX_LAST_MASK 0x0800U        /*!< Last BD of the frame mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_TX_TRANMITCRC_MASK 0x0400U  /*!< Transmit CRC mask. */
/*@}*/

/* Extended control regions for enhanced buffer descriptors. */
#ifdef ENET_ENHANCEDBUFFERDESCRIPTOR_MODE
/*! @name First extended control region bit masks of the receive buffer descriptor. */
/*@{*/
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_IPV4_MASK 0x0001U             /*!< Ipv4 frame mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_IPV6_MASK 0x0002U             /*!< Ipv6 frame mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_VLAN_MASK 0x0004U             /*!< VLAN frame mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_PROTOCOLCHECKSUM_MASK 0x0010U /*!< Protocol checksum error mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_IPHEADCHECKSUM_MASK 0x0020U   /*!< IP header checksum error mask. */
/*@}*/

/*! @name Second extended control region bit masks of the receive buffer descriptor. */
/*@{*/
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_INTERRUPT_MASK 0x0080U /*!< BD interrupt mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_UNICAST_MASK 0x0100U   /*!< Unicast frame mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_COLLISION_MASK 0x0200U /*!< BD collision mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_PHYERR_MASK 0x0400U    /*!< PHY error mask. */
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_RX_MACERR_MASK 0x8000U    /*!< Mac error mask. */
/*@}*/

/*! @name First extended control region bit masks of the transmit buffer descriptor. */
/*@{*/
#define ENET_BUFFDESCRIPTOR_TX_ERR_MASK 0x8000U              /*!< Transmit error mask. */

Let me search for the ENET_Reset function in the header file:

<bash>grep -n "ENET_Reset" /repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.h</bash>

Exit code: 0
600:static inline void ENET_Reset(ENET_Type *base)

Let me look at the ENET_Reset function implementation:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.h</str_replace_editor> [595, 610]
Replacement successful in /repo/targets/TARGET_Freescale/TARGET_MCUXpresso_MCUS/TARGET_MCU_K64F/drivers/fsl_enet.c. Found 1 occurrences

The fix I've implemented addresses the peripheral initialization order issue in the ENET_Deinit function. The problem was that the Ethernet module was not being properly reset before disabling the clock, which could leave the module in an inconsistent state and cause dependent hardware to malfunction.

The key changes made:

1. Added `ENET_Reset(base);` call before disabling the clock in the ENET_Deinit function
2. This ensures the module is properly reset to a clean state before the clock is disabled
3. This matches the initialization pattern where the module is reset after clock enable

This fix ensures that:
- The Ethernet module is properly reset during deinitialization
- Dependent hardware will not malfunction due to the module being left in an inconsistent state
- The deinitialization sequence now properly mirrors the initialization sequence

SUBMIT
Agent submitted.
